---
title: "ASD Data"
author: "Jackson Strand"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r}
library(spectrolab)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyverse)

# Tell R the path to your folder with asd data
data_path <- "SpectroLab/data_in/"
files <- list.files(data_path, pattern = "\\.asd$", recursive = TRUE, full.names = TRUE)

# Load that data in!
spec <- read_spectra(files, format = "asd")

# clean up the ends
spec <- spec[, 400:2400] # only take the wvls from 400-2400
# water_bands <- (1350:1460, 1790:1960) 
# spec <- spec[, !wavelengths(spec) %in% water_bands] # optional clean up of atmospheric bands

# Get an interactive plot window!
# plot_interactive(spec) # You should be able to see the 2 samples... but there are still 5 files for each...

# Create a dataframe 
spec_df <- as.data.frame(spec, metadata = TRUE)

# average the 5 files per sample
spec_avg <- spec_df %>% 
  mutate(ID = gsub("_.*", "", sample_name)) %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols = -c(sample_name, ID),
               names_to = "wavelength", 
               values_to = "reflectance") %>% 
  mutate(wavelength = as.numeric(wavelength)) %>% 
  group_by(ID, wavelength) %>% 
  summarise(mean_refl = mean(reflectance, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")
```


```{r}
source("SpectroLab/scripts/clean_ids.R")

spec_avg_clean <- clean_spec_ids(spec_avg) %>% 
  filter(rep != "E")

# Pivot to wide format first
spec_wide <- spec_avg_clean %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = wavelength, values_from = mean_refl, names_prefix = "w")

spec_wide <- spec_wide %>%
  mutate(
    NDVI  = (w800 - w670) / (w800 + w670),
    PRI   = (w531 - w570) / (w531 + w570),   # photochemical reflectance, stress-sensitive
    WBI   = w900 / w970,                       # water band index
    SIPI  = (w800 - w445) / (w800 - w680)     # structure-insensitive pigment index
  )

```

```{r}
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)

# Mixed model: treatment + species as fixed effects, plant as random (repeated measures)
m1 <- lmer(SIPI ~ treatment * species * week + (1 | plant_num), data = spec_wide)
summary(m1)
anova(m1)
```

```{r}
library(emmeans)

m_pri <- lmer(PRI ~ treatment * species * week + (1 | plant_num), data = spec_wide)

# Compare infested vs control within each species
emmeans(m_pri, pairwise ~ treatment | species)

# Or visualize it
emmip(m_pri, treatment ~ week | species, CIs = TRUE)
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)

emm_df <- as.data.frame(emmeans(m_pri, ~ treatment | species))

ggplot(emm_df, aes(x = species, y = emmean, fill = treatment)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(0.7), width = 0.6) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lower.CL, ymax = upper.CL),
                position = position_dodge(0.7), width = 0.2) +
  annotate("text", x = 2, y = max(emm_df$upper.CL) + 0.002, label = "*", size = 6) +
  labs(x = "Species", y = "PRI (mean ± 95% CI)",
       title = "PRI by Treatment and Species") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("d" = "Dalmatian", "y" = "Yellow")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("control" = "#7fbfff", "infested" = "#ff7f7f"),
                    labels = c("Control", "Infested")) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}

spec_wide_nona <-
  spec_wide %>% 
  na.omit()



library(vegan)

# Build spectral matrix — wavelength columns only
spec_matrix <- spec_wide_nona %>% 
  select(starts_with("w") & !matches("WBI|week")) %>%  # exclude WBI and week_num cols
  as.matrix()

# Metadata
meta <- spec_wide_nona %>%
  select(rep_id, species, treatment, week)

# ---- PERMANOVA ----
perm_full <- adonis2(spec_matrix ~ treatment * species * week,
                     data = meta,
                     permutations = 999,
                     method = "euclidean",
                     by = "margin")  # Type III — tests each term controlling for others
print(perm_full)

# ---- Check dispersion assumption ----
dist_mat <- vegdist(spec_matrix, method = "euclidean")

disp_treatment <- betadisper(dist_mat, meta$treatment)
anova(disp_treatment)  # should be NS for PERMANOVA to be trustworthy

disp_species <- betadisper(dist_mat, meta$species)
anova(disp_species)

# ---- PCA ordination ----
pca_result <- rda(spec_matrix)

pca_scores <- as.data.frame(scores(pca_result, display = "sites")) %>%
  bind_cols(meta)

# Variance explained
pct <- summary(pca_result)$cont$importance
pc1_var <- round(pct[2, 1] * 100, 1)
pc2_var <- round(pct[2, 2] * 100, 1)

ggplot(pca_scores, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2,
                        color = treatment,
                        shape = species)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
  stat_ellipse(aes(group = interaction(treatment, species)),
               level = 0.95, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("control" = "#4477AA", "infested" = "#CC3311")) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("d" = 16, "y" = 17),
                     labels = c("Dalmatian", "Yellow")) +
  labs(title = "PCA of full reflectance spectra (400–2400nm)",
       x = paste0("PC1 (", pc1_var, "%)"),
       y = paste0("PC2 (", pc2_var, "%)"),
       color = "Treatment", shape = "Species") +
  theme_minimal()







perm_full <- adonis2(spec_matrix ~ treatment * species * week,
                     data = meta,
                     permutations = 999,
                     method = "euclidean",
                     by = "terms")  # sequential — change from "margin"
print(perm_full)
```




```{r}
spec_avg_clean %>%
  group_by(wavelength, species, treatment) %>%
  summarise(mean = mean(mean_refl), se = sd(mean_refl)/sqrt(n())) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = wavelength, y = mean, color = treatment, fill = treatment)) +
    geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = mean - se, ymax = mean + se), alpha = 0.2, color = NA) +
    geom_line() +
    facet_wrap(~species) +
    labs(title = "Mean reflectance by treatment and species",
         x = "Wavelength (nm)", y = "Reflectance") +
    theme_minimal()
```
NDVI  
PRI    
WBI   
SIPI
```{r}
# Your IDs like "d1c", "y2i" — the number is the plant/rep within species
# You should already have plant_num, but let's make a clear rep ID
spec_wide <- spec_wide %>%
  mutate(rep_id = paste0(species, plant_num))  # e.g. "d1", "y2"

library(lmerTest)

# Convert week to numeric for slope interpretation
spec_wide <- spec_wide %>%
  mutate(week_num = case_when(
    week == "wk1" ~ 1,
    week == "wk2" ~ 2,
    week == "wk4" ~ 4
  ))

# Random slope model — allows each rep to have its own trajectory
m_slope <- lmer(SIPI ~ treatment * species * week_num + 
                  (week_num | rep_id),   # rep-specific slopes over time
                data = spec_wide)

anova(m_slope)
```


```{r}
# Reshape indices to long for easy faceting
library(tidyr)

spec_long_indices <- spec_wide %>%
  select(rep_id, species, treatment, week_num, week, PRI, NDVI, WBI, SIPI) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(PRI, NDVI, WBI, SIPI), 
               names_to = "index", values_to = "value")

ggplot(spec_long_indices, aes(x = week_num, y = value, 
                               color = treatment,
                               group = interaction(rep_id, treatment))) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  facet_grid(index ~ species, scales = "free_y",
             labeller = labeller(species = c("d" = "Dalmatian", "y" = "Yellow"))) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("control" = "#4477AA", "infested" = "#CC3311")) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(1, 2, 4), labels = c("Wk1", "Wk2", "Wk4")) +
  labs(title = "Spectral index trajectories by rep",
       x = "Week", y = "Index value", color = "Treatment") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"))
```
